name: Delivered electricity
basis: electrical work at delivery boundary
calculation: ideal electrical work potential per unit electrical energy
adoption_note: Use for grid electricity, site electricity, and metered electrical delivery; report conversion losses separately.

name: Mechanical shaft work
basis: shaft work at machine boundary
calculation: ideal work potential per unit work
adoption_note: Use for motors, turbines, pumps, and mechanical drives when reporting useful shaft work.

name: PV DC electrical output
basis: DC electrical output after PV conversion
calculation: electrical work potential per unit electrical energy
adoption_note: Use for PV output after conversion; solar resource exergy and conversion efficiency are separate questions.

name: Solar radiation resource at 20 C reference
basis: Petela radiation Exergy Factor, reference=20 C
adoption_note: Use when reporting incident solar resource quality before PV or thermal conversion losses.
source: Petela radiation factor using DiMurro 2026 default reference

name: Battery discharge electricity
basis: electrical output boundary
reference: battery AC or DC output boundary
calculation: electrical work potential per unit delivered electricity
adoption_note: Use for delivered battery electricity; round-trip efficiency and degradation belong in process metrics.

name: Pumped hydro electrical output
basis: electrical output boundary
calculation: potential energy converted to electrical work at output boundary
adoption_note: Use for delivered electricity; hydraulic and turbine losses are reported separately.

name: 35 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=35 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for very low-grade waste heat, data-center heat, and mild return streams when 20 C is the declared sink.

name: 40 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=40 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for low-temperature space heat and low-grade recovery screening.

name: 50 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=50 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for warm-water loops, preheat streams, and moderate building heating services.

name: 60 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=60 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for domestic hot water and moderate process preheat examples.

name: 70 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=70 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for district heat and process hot-water streams with a 20 C reference sink.

name: 70 C heat to 35 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=70 C, sink=35 C
adoption_note: Use to show how a warmer accessible sink lowers the same stream's work potential.

name: 80 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=80 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for district heating and recovered hot-water examples.

name: 80 C district heat to 50 C return
basis: Carnot factor, source=80 C, sink=50 C
adoption_note: Use when the actual accessible sink is the return line rather than ambient conditions.

name: 90 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=90 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for higher-temperature district heating and process hot-water screening.

name: 90 C district heat to 50 C return
basis: Carnot factor, source=90 C, sink=50 C
adoption_note: Use for district heating records where the return temperature is the operational sink.

name: 120 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=120 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for industrial drying, sterilization-adjacent services, and higher-grade waste heat.

name: 150 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=150 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for low-pressure steam, process heat, and thermal storage examples.

name: 180 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=180 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for process steam and mid-temperature industrial heat screening.

name: 250 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=250 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for higher-grade process heat and industrial heat-recovery ranking.

name: 500 C heat to 20 C sink
basis: Carnot factor, source=500 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for high-temperature process heat examples where a 20 C sink is declared.

name: 7 C cooling service against 30 C ambient
basis: minimum work potential per unit cooling service, cold=7 C, ambient=30 C
adoption_note: Use for chilled water, refrigeration, cold storage, and district cooling demand records.

name: 5 C cooling service against 20 C ambient
basis: minimum work potential per unit cooling service, cold=5 C, ambient=20 C
adoption_note: Use for the public web comparison preset for low-temperature cooling service.

name: Methane on LHV basis
basis: chemical exergy divided by lower heating value
adoption_note: Use when methane or natural gas is reported on an LHV basis; declare composition for real inventories.

name: Methane on HHV basis
basis: chemical exergy divided by higher heating value
adoption_note: Use to avoid mixing HHV energy reports with LHV exergy-factor assumptions.

name: Hydrogen on LHV basis
basis: chemical exergy divided by lower heating value
adoption_note: Use for electrolyzer output, hydrogen storage, and fuel policy comparisons on an LHV basis.

name: Hydrogen on HHV basis
basis: chemical exergy divided by higher heating value
adoption_note: Use when hydrogen quantities are reported on an HHV basis; do not compare directly with LHV records without converting.

name: Diesel on LHV basis
basis: approximate chemical exergy divided by lower heating value
adoption_note: Use as an illustrative public web preset; use composition-specific values for engineering studies.
source: Exergy Factor website approximate fuel preset

name: Gasoline on LHV basis
basis: approximate chemical exergy divided by lower heating value
adoption_note: Use as an illustrative public web preset; use composition-specific values for engineering studies.
source: Exergy Factor website approximate fuel preset

name: Crude oil approximate chemical exergy factor
basis: approximate crude oil chemical exergy factor
calculation: approximate chemical exergy / declared energy basis, rounded
adoption_note: Use as an illustrative public web preset; use crude-assay-specific values for engineering studies.
source: Exergy Factor website approximate fuel preset

name: Coal on LHV basis
basis: approximate chemical exergy divided by lower heating value
adoption_note: Use as an illustrative public web preset; use composition-specific values for engineering studies.
source: Exergy Factor website approximate fuel preset

name: Hot-water storage at 80 C
basis: Carnot factor, storage temperature=80 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use when comparing thermal storage against electric or chemical storage by useful work potential.

name: Thermal storage at 150 C
basis: Carnot factor, storage temperature=150 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use for molten salt, thermal oil, or process-heat storage examples with declared sink conditions.

name: Electricity savings in M&V
basis: avoided delivered electricity
calculation: Delta X_A = Delta E for delivered electricity
adoption_note: Use in IPMVP-style reports to show that 1 MWh electric savings avoids about 1 MWh_ex.

name: 40 C heat savings in M&V
basis: Carnot factor, source=40 C, sink=20 C
adoption_note: Use to distinguish low-temperature heat savings from equal-MWh electricity savings.
